evidence: frontmatter description reads 'AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md'; body h1 reads 'Keep AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, and AGENTS.md in sync'; step 1 runs 'git diff HEAD -- AGENTS.md GEMINI.md AGENTS.md' — CLAUDE.md absent in all three places; .claude/skills/ copy correctly lists CLAUDE.md
description: CLAUDE.md is replaced by a second AGENTS.md in both the frontmatter description and Step 1 git diff command; the skill will never detect changes to CLAUDE.md
suggested_fix: Replace both occurrences of the duplicate AGENTS.md with CLAUDE.md to match the .claude/skills/ copy

evidence: frontmatter block contains only 'description' and 'allowed-tools'; no 'name' field present
description: Missing required 'name' frontmatter field; Claude Code plugin commands require name for slash-command registration
suggested_fix: Add 'name: restart' (or the intended command name) to the frontmatter

evidence: frontmatter block contains 'description' and 'argument-hint' and 'allowed-tools' but no 'name' field

suggested_fix: Add 'name: learn' to the frontmatter

evidence: urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=15) at line 77 makes outbound HTTP GET to https://pypi.org/pypi/reflexio-ai/<version>/json
description: Outbound network request to PyPI; URL is constructed from package-controlled version string read from a local pyproject.toml; not directly user-injectable but documents an external dependency
suggested_fix: Document network requirement in function docstring; add --no-pypi-check flag option for air-gapped environments

evidence: tar.extractall(vendor_dest) at line 138 with no member filter; Python 3.12 deprecates extractall without filter argument
description: tarfile.extractall without member filter; theoretical path-traversal if tarball contained '../' entries; mitigated because source is git archive on a local trusted repo which never produces such entries
suggested_fix: Add filter='data' argument: tar.extractall(vendor_dest, filter='data') — requires Python 3.12+, or add manual member path validation for earlier versions

evidence: append_env_value REFLEXIO_API_KEY writes API key to ~/.reflexio/.env in plaintext at lines 343-344; chmod 600 applied immediately after
description: API key stored in plaintext .env file; mitigated by chmod 600 and the use of env_quote escaping; risk is low in practice but auditing tools will flag plaintext credential storage
suggested_fix: Consider documenting the chmod 600 protection in user-facing docs; current implementation is acceptable for CLI tooling

evidence: command string contains: _R=$(ls -dt "$HOME/.codex/plugins/cache/reflexioai/claude-smart"/*/ 2>/dev/null | head -n 1); [ -n "$_R" ] && . "${_R%/}/scripts/_codex_env.sh"
description: Plugin root discovered via ls pipe and the result is source'd; if the .codex/plugins/ cache directory were compromised an attacker could substitute an arbitrary script; risk is low because the directory is user-local
suggested_fix: Prefer explicit CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or PLUGIN_ROOT (already tried first); add a path-integrity check before sourcing

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools; consumers cannot determine tool scope without reading full body
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read, Write, Edit' or equivalent to frontmatter

evidence: 'Stage changes — run git add <files> for relevant modified/untracked files'
description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' leaves undefined which files should be staged
suggested_fix: Replace 'relevant modified/untracked files' with an explicit criterion, e.g. 'all modified tracked files and new untracked files except .env and gitignored paths'

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools declaration to frontmatter

evidence: 'Provide context and background with links to relevant issues/docs'
description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in body guideline
suggested_fix: Specify what makes an issue/doc relevant or remove the qualifier

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools

evidence: 'Only report findings that are actionable'
description: Vague quantifier 'actionable' — actionable is itself undefined
suggested_fix: Define 'actionable' concretely, e.g. 'only report findings where a specific code change is possible'

evidence: 'Is retry logic appropriate and bounded?'
description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in review checklist item 3.5
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete criterion, e.g. 'Is retry count capped (≤3 retries) and does it use exponential backoff?'

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools declaration to frontmatter

evidence: no instruction to stop and inform the user when 'git status' shows nothing to commit; Step 7 'Attempt commit' will fail silently
description: Command does not handle the case where there are no staged or unstaged changes to commit
suggested_fix: Add a guard after Step 1: if git status shows nothing staged or unstaged, inform the user and stop

evidence: 'Stage files - Add relevant untracked/modified files if needed'
description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' in Step 3 leaves undefined which untracked files should be staged
suggested_fix: Specify criteria for what to stage, e.g. 'all modified tracked files and new source files; never .env or files matching .gitignore'

evidence: no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
description: Command does not declare allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools declaration to frontmatter

evidence: 'Create detailed Product Requirements Documents that are clear, actionable, and suitable for implementation'
description: Vague quantifier 'suitable' in opening body description
suggested_fix: Remove 'suitable for implementation' or replace with a concrete quality bar

evidence: 'Create detailed Product Requirements Documents that are clear, actionable, and suitable for implementation'

evidence: .agents/skills/sync-agent-instructions/SKILL.md lists AGENTS.md twice while .claude/skills/sync-agent-instructions/SKILL.md lists CLAUDE.md correctly; AGENTS.md declares .agents/skills/ is a symlink to .claude/skills/ but the files differ
description: .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/ are documented as symlinked but are separate diverged copies; sync-agent-instructions has a substantive content difference (CLAUDE.md vs AGENTS.md)
suggested_fix: Convert .agents/skills/ to a real symlink pointing to .claude/skills/ to prevent future divergence, or add a CI check that enforces byte-for-byte equality

evidence: plugin.json contains name/version/description/author/keywords only; no hooks, skills, or commands keys; hooks are at plugin/hooks/hooks.json and commands at plugin/commands/*.md
description: Plugin manifest does not cross-reference hooks, skills, or commands; auto-discovery may compensate but the manifest cannot be used for independent validation
suggested_fix: Add explicit 'hooks', 'skills', and 'commands' arrays to plugin.json referencing the actual file paths
